security_gap.gap_description: Vote input may be spoofed without signature verification.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system accepts unsigned votes from external actors.

assumptions: The governance contract does not enforce signature verification for vote messages.
The actor is able to access the voting interface without authentication.
A malicious actor can generate valid-looking vote data without authorization

mitigation.strategy: Require signature-based vote authentication and role validation.

description: An attacker may impersonate a user and submit fraudulent votes to the governance contract.

security_gap.gap_description: Vote data may be tampered with during transmission or processing.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system lacks sufficient input validation to prevent manipulation of vote data.

assumptions: The actor can intercept and modify the vote data in transit.
The system does not perform integrity checks on incoming vote data.
A malicious actor can modify vote data without detection

mitigation.strategy: Implement data integrity checks (e.g., checksums, digital signatures) and input validation.

description: An attacker could alter or manipulate the vote data, potentially influencing the outcome of governance proposals.

security_gap.gap_description: Vote submission lacks mechanisms to ensure non-repudiation.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system does not provide a mechanism for verifying the authenticity of submitted votes.

assumptions: The system does not maintain audit logs of vote submissions.
The actor can submit votes anonymously without leaving any trace.
A malicious actor can deny their involvement in a vote

mitigation.strategy: Implement digital signatures and audit logging for vote submissions.

description: An attacker who submits fraudulent votes could plausibly deny their actions.

security_gap.gap_description: The governance contract may not be resilient against flood attacks.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system may not be designed to handle large volumes of voting requests efficiently.

assumptions: The governance contract does not employ rate limiting or other anti-DoS measures.
A malicious actor can flood the system with requests.
A large number of requests could overwhelm the governance contract

mitigation.strategy: Implement rate limiting and other anti-DoS measures for vote submissions.

description: An attacker could flood the governance contract with vote submissions, rendering it unavailable to legitimate users.
